Plains (1) ACCEPT type for specie

An explanation that includes the following points: • allopatric speciation (1) • (allopatric) because lemurs have the same common ancestor isolated from those in Africa (1) • sympatric speciation (1) • (sympatric) because Sifakas and Indri live together but have a different diet (1) ACCEPT geographic speciation ACCEPT description of how diet is different (4) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An explanation that includes two of the following points: • because only small quantities of DNA can be collected (1) • therefore need to {amplify / increase the number of copies of} DNA (1) • so that there is enough to {run on gel electrophoresis / analyse} (1) ACCEPT collecting a sample is difficult (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An explanation that includes two of the following points: • gel electrophoresis run on DNA from both groups of lemurs (1) • pattern of bands were similar showing the lemurs were genetically similar (1) • base sequencing would show similar sequences (1) ACCEPT use of bioinformatics to show that the base sequences were similar (2 ) number Answer Mark

The only correct answer is C. A is incorrect because dendrochronology is dating trees using tree rings B is incorrect because epigenetics studies changes in gene expression D is incorrect because species diversity is a measure of the number of different species in an area (1) number Answer Additional guidance Mark • 0.12 (1) • mm hr -1 (1) NB If different units have been used, award a correct numerical value e.g. 0.012 cm hr -1 = 2 marks (2) number Answer Additional guidance Mark An answer that includes three of the following points: • suitable as a mean is calculated (1) • but there is no indication of sample size so may not be that suitable (1) • data looks more suitable in the first 45 hours as the error bars are small (1) • may not be suitable as the incubation temperature may be different to body temperature (1) • will not be suitable if person died more than 120 hours ago (1) ACCEPT {reliable / accurate} as an alternative for suitable throughout ACCEPT overall the error bars are (quite) small parts of the graph where the error bars do not overlap are suitable / converse ACCEPT body temperature does not remain constant (3) number Answer Additional guidance Mark A description that includes three of the following points: • (start the investigation with) several blowfly eggs (1) • measure the length of larvae (at intervals) over a period of 120 hours (1) • credit a named control variable appropriate to this data collection (1) • calculate mean and {standard deviation / range bars / error bars} (1) e.g. same food supply, species of blowfly IGNORE inappropriate named control variables eg pH (3) number Answer Additional guidance Mark

An answer that includes four of the following points: THREE FROM: • body temperature is readily available information (1) • {calibration curves / formulae} available to work backwards to estimate time of death (1) • but the change in body temperature is due to several factors (1) • credit example of one of these factors (1) AND • therefore of limited use unless used in conjunction with other methods (1) ACCEPT calculations can be made to estimate time of death e.g. ambient temperature ACCEPT on its own (4 ) number Answer Mark

A is incorrect because pH is a log scale so the difference of 2 is 100 fold B is incorrect because pH is a log scale so the difference of 2 is 100 fold C is incorrect because there are fewer hydrogen ions at pH 8.5 than pH 6.5 (1) C
